summary: Claude via the REAL mcpbridge → executor → real execute_shell_command (cat a pre-seeded secret), streamed at the mcpagent layer

agent_review.notes: PRODUCTION-FIDELITY PROOF: the turn ran the REAL path — Claude Code → real mcpbridge binary → executor HTTP API → real execute_shell_command (actual shell) → `cat secret.txt`. Evidence: tool_names=[mcp__api-bridge__execute_shell_command] streamed as a ToolCallStartEvent; the answer REALBRIDGE_SECRET_ff8622c7a41d existed ONLY in a pre-seeded file, never in the prompt, so it was impossible to answer without executing the tool through the bridge. The earlier terminal/transcript-mixing finding is now FIXED: StreamingChunkEvent.Source separates terminal frames from clean content, so the no-terminal view (Source != terminal) is exactly the two genuine assistant messages — 'I'll read the secret file from the project directory.' and the secret — with zero ANSI/terminal noise (asserted on the live chunks). Clean end to end.
